title: ranunculus sulphureus subsp. taimyrensis
intro: Ranunculus sulphureus subsp. taimyrensis, also known as the Taimyr buttercup, is a rare and endangered subspecies of flowering plant in the buttercup family. It is a perennial plant with yellow flowers and has been traditionally used in medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's roots and rhizomes have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its medicinal properties such as flavonoids and alkaloids. Herbal preparations of the Taimyr buttercup include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to support health and well-being, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
